T0503.definition: Additional profit extracted by miners or validators from blockchain users by reordering, inserting, or censoring transactions.
T0503.description: MEV arises from block producers' control over transaction ordering within a block. Common forms include frontrunning, sandwich attacks (inserting transactions before and after a target transaction), and arbitrage opportunity capture. While MEV can improve market efficiency by incentivizing arbitrageurs to correct price discrepancies across decentralized exchanges, it also introduces significant risks to blockchain consensus security and fairness.
T0503.usageExample: A bot detects a user's large buy order, frontruns it by purchasing the asset at a lower price, and then sells immediately after the user's transaction drives up the price, profiting from the price impact.

references.title: Ethereum is a Dark Forest - Paradigm

T0503.title: Maximal Extractable Value
